title: Diversion risk: Raspberry Pi 4B found in Geran-2/3 (Shahed-136/238)
detail: GUR teardown confirmed Raspberry Pi 4B used as 'Borscht Tracker V3' in Geran-2/3 (Shahed-136/238). 40,000+ units reportedly diverted. Forge DB contains 'DroneEngage — Cloud-Connected MAVLink Companion Platform' using same silicon family — shared supply chain with verified diversion pipeline.
prediction: Gray market premium persists. DRAM-driven price hikes may slow diversion as cost-benefit shifts, but active procurement pipelines remain.

title: Diversion risk: Raspberry Pi found in Geran-5
detail: GUR teardown confirmed Raspberry Pi used as 'Tracker + 3G/4G modem' in Geran-5. Unknown quantity units reportedly diverted. Forge DB contains 'DroneEngage — Cloud-Connected MAVLink Companion Platform' using same silicon family — shared supply chain with verified diversion pipeline.
prediction: Gray market premium persists. DRAM-driven price hikes may slow diversion as cost-benefit shifts, but active procurement pipelines remain.

title: Diversion risk: Raspberry Pi 5 found in Molniya-2R
detail: GUR teardown confirmed Raspberry Pi 5 used as 'Reconnaissance computer' in Molniya-2R. Unknown quantity units reportedly diverted. Forge DB contains 'DroneEngage — Cloud-Connected MAVLink Companion Platform' using same silicon family — shared supply chain with verified diversion pipeline.
prediction: Gray market premium persists. DRAM-driven price hikes may slow diversion as cost-benefit shifts, but active procurement pipelines remain.

title: RPi 4B confirmed in Geran-2/3 'Borscht' tracker — 40,000+ units diverted
detail: GUR War & Sanctions portal and Ukrainian military intelligence confirmed Raspberry Pi 4B as the core of the 'Borscht Tracker V3' in Geran-2 (Shahed-136) and Geran-3 (Shahed-238) attack drones. Reports indicate 40,000+ units procured via gray market channels. RPi CEO Eben Upton acknowledged use in Russian drones and tightened end-use requirements for China sales. Cross-reference: RPi CM4 used by Freefly Astro (Blue UAS) and custom ArduPilot builds.
prediction: RPi gray market premium in China remains elevated (30-50% above MSRP). Active procurement pipeline confirmed by multiple intelligence sources.

title: RPi 5 confirmed in Molniya-2R reconnaissance drone
detail: GUR documented RPi 5 integrated alongside Chinese Mini PC F8 (rebranded as 'Raskat') running Windows 11 in the Molniya-2R reconnaissance UAV. The Molniya-2R is the recon variant of the kamikaze FPV drone, used for monitoring Ukrainian positions and strike adjustment. Confirms active, ongoing procurement of latest-gen RPi hardware despite tightened export controls.
prediction: RPi gray market premium in China remains elevated (30-50% above MSRP). Active procurement pipeline confirmed by multiple intelligence sources.

title: RPi tracker with mesh networking in Geran-5 jet drone
detail: Russia's Geran-5 (6m wingspan, jet-powered, 90kg warhead, 1000km range) uses a 12-channel Kometa satellite navigation system and a tracker based on RPi with 3G/4G modems. The Geran-5 also features XK-F358 mesh network modems enabling 'chain control' — multiple drones in a salvo maintaining comms. This represents escalation from simple GPS-guided to mesh-networked swarm capability, all built on commercially available SBCs.
prediction: RPi gray market premium in China remains elevated (30-50% above MSRP). Active procurement pipeline confirmed by multiple intelligence sources.

title: Geographic risk: Taiwan — high risk, critical to Blue UAS supply chain
detail: Taiwan: Cross-strait tensions. TSMC concentration. Single point of failure for all advanced chips. Critical components: Qualcomm QRB5165 (fab), TSMC node for Jetson, Ambarella SoCs. Affected Blue UAS platforms: All Jetson-based, All QRB5165-based, All Ambarella-based.
prediction: Any disruption in Taiwan cascades to Qualcomm QRB5165 (fab), TSMC node for Jetson supply for all Blue UAS programs.

title: US-manufactured mesh radios: 14 of 24 options — NDAA bottleneck
detail: Forge DB tracks 24 mesh radios. Only 14 from US manufacturers. Blue UAS platforms require NDAA-compliant mesh for multi-vehicle ops. Doodle Labs and Silvus dominate the Blue UAS mesh market — limited supplier diversity creates concentration risk.
prediction: Mesh radio becomes supply bottleneck as multi-drone programs (Lattice, Replicator) scale. Limited alternatives at MANET performance tier.

title: Shield AI Hivemind (Shield AI) — combat-deployed, 5 active contracts
detail: Shield AI Hivemind: AI pilot + swarm coordination. EdgeOS middleware, Hivemind Pilot, Commander C2. GPS/comms-denied capable. Combat status: 200+ flights in Ukraine on V-BAT, 200+ Russian targets identified. Contracts: USAF CCA Increment 1 (YFQ-44A), AFSOC STRATFI, Ukraine Brave1, Taiwan NCSIST. Hardware dependencies: NVIDIA Jetson Orin, Hivemind ASIC. Integrated on: Shield AI V-BAT, Anduril YFQ-44A (Fury CCA), MQ-20 Avenger, Destinus Hornet.
prediction: Contract wins for Shield AI drive hardware demand for NVIDIA Jetson Orin, Hivemind ASIC. Monitor distributor lead times.

title: Anduril Lattice (Anduril Industries) — combat-deployed, 4 active contracts
detail: Anduril Lattice: AI-powered C2 and sensor fusion platform. Connects autonomous systems into unified kill chain. Mesh networking, threat detection. Combat status: Deployed in USCENTCOM, USINDOPACOM theaters. Contracts: Replicator Phase 2, USSOCOM MFWS, Lattice Mesh network, Taiwan integration. Hardware dependencies: NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin, Lattice CertusPro-NX FPGA, Doodle Labs mesh radios. Integrated on: Anduril Ghost-X, Anduril Altius, Anduril Anvil (C-UAS), Sentry Tower.
prediction: Contract wins for Anduril Industries drive hardware demand for NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin, Lattice CertusPro-NX FPGA. Monitor distributor lead times.

title: Policy: FCC blocks new foreign drone imports (equipment authorization freeze)
detail: Date: 2025-12. Domestic Blue UAS demand spike — no new Chinese drone models can enter market Affected manufacturers: All Blue UAS manufacturers (positive), DJI/Autel (negative). Component pressure: Jetson Orin NX, QRB5165, STM32H7.
prediction: Demand shift toward domestic/allied components. Monitor lead times for Jetson Orin NX, QRB5165.

title: Policy: Drone Dominance executive order — 'Unleashing U.S. Military Drone Domi
detail: Date: 2025-07. Massive defense FPV procurement acceleration. Blue UAS list transitions to DCMA. Affected manufacturers: Neros, Teal, ModalAI, All FPV component suppliers. Component pressure: STM32H7, ESP32-S3, ELRS modules, FLIR thermals.
prediction: Demand shift toward domestic/allied components. Monitor lead times for STM32H7, ESP32-S3.

title: Policy: DRAM tariff & AI datacenter priority — memory allocation shift
detail: Date: 2025-2026. AI datacenters get DRAM priority. SBC makers (RPi, embedded) pay more, wait longer. Affected manufacturers: RPi, All SBC manufacturers, Embedded system builders. Component pressure: RPi CM4/CM5, Jetson modules, LPDDR4X.
prediction: Demand shift toward domestic/allied components. Monitor lead times for RPi CM4/CM5, Jetson modules.

title: Policy: US tariff escalation — 25%+ on Chinese electronics components
detail: Date: 2026. Components sourced from China cost more. NDAA compliance pressure increases. Affected manufacturers: Any platform with Chinese-origin components, Autel (CN/US hybrid). Component pressure: Motors (many CN-made), ESCs, Frames, Batteries.
prediction: Demand shift toward domestic/allied components. Monitor lead times for Motors (many CN-made), ESCs.

title: Blue UAS BOM cost index: $4,348 — 720% premium over non-compliant build
detail: Reference Blue UAS ISR quad BOM: $4,348. Equivalent non-compliant build: $530. Compliance premium: 720%. Largest cost drivers: mesh radio ($1,800), companion computer ($599), frame ($400). DRAM pricing and tariffs push this index higher each quarter.
prediction: BOM index trending up. DRAM + tariffs add ~8-15% YoY. At $4,348, cost barrier limits Blue UAS adoption by smaller agencies.

title: Neros Technologies: 2500/mo capacity with 50 employees — lean operation
detail: Neros Technologies (Los Angeles, CA): ~2500 units/mo with 50 employees (50 units/employee/mo). Facilities: LA manufacturing. Signal: Drone Dominance FPV at scale. 2,200+/mo claimed. High output-per-employee ratio suggests limited surge capacity.
prediction: Production scaling risk if contract demand exceeds capacity. Workforce expansion or second shift needed.

title: DRAM pricing: +8.5% QoQ, +22% YoY — cascading to SBC costs
detail: DDR4 8Gb contract: $3.25/unit. LPDDR4X: $3.8/unit. QoQ increase: 8.5%. Driver: AI datacenter demand consuming production capacity. Impact: RPi 5 8GB: $80→$95, 16GB: $120→$145. Orin NX 16GB: $599→$649. Forecast: Elevated through 2027, possible easing 2028. Morgan Stanley projects $620B AI infrastructure spend in 2026 (up from $470B in 2025).
prediction: DRAM pressure continues through 2027. Every SBC and companion computer in the Forge DB is affected. No relief until new fab capacity comes online ~2028.

title: Geographic risk: Israel — medium risk (Hailo AI accelerators, D-Fend C-UAS)
detail: Israel: Strong ally but regional instability. Hailo-8/10H AI chips used in drone vision systems. Critical components: Hailo AI accelerators, D-Fend C-UAS, Various defense components.
prediction: Monitor Israel stability. Component sourcing diversification recommended.

title: Geographic risk: South Korea — medium risk (Samsung DRAM, SK Hynix memory)
detail: South Korea: Major DRAM supplier. Samsung/SK Hynix produce majority of global DRAM. North Korea tensions. Critical components: Samsung DRAM, SK Hynix memory, Samsung foundry.
prediction: Monitor South Korea stability. Component sourcing diversification recommended.

prediction: Monitor Qualcomm QRB5165 chip availability. Any fab disruption or allocation shift cascades to 5 companion computer products.

title: C-UAS and UAS share SDR modules supply — 2 C-UAS companies competing

prediction: C-UAS + UAS compound demand on SDR modules. Monitor as C-UAS contracts scale in 2026.

title: Policy: American Security Drone Act (ASDA) — bans DJI for federal use
detail: Date: 2024. Federal agencies must transition to Blue UAS. Creates demand cliff for DJI, ramp for domestic. Affected manufacturers: DJI (banned), All Blue UAS manufacturers (demand surge). Component pressure: All Blue UAS companion computers, NDAA-compliant radios.
prediction: Demand shift toward domestic/allied components. Monitor lead times for All Blue UAS companion computers, NDAA-compliant radios.

title: Mesh radio is 41% of Blue UAS BOM — single largest cost driver
detail: Doodle Labs entry-level mesh: $1,800. Silvus MN Micro: $4,500-5,200. This single component is 41% of the total $4,348 BOM. No low-cost NDAA-compliant alternative exists at MANET performance tier.
prediction: Mesh radio cost is the biggest barrier to Blue UAS fleet scaling. Market opportunity for lower-cost NDAA mesh entrants.
